data.title: A whale transferred 2,000 ETH to an exchange. If it sold, it would lose $320,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain data analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the giant whale address that has built a position of 4026.47 ETH since June 10 transferred 2000 ETH to the exchange four hours ago, which is about 4.87 million US dollars at the current price. The recharge time of this address is almost the same as the starting point of ETH's rise this morning.
The average cost of building a position for this batch of ETH is US$2,598. The selling price this time is lower than the cost price, and the estimated loss is about US$324,000. The remaining 2026 ETH held is still floating at a loss of about US$178,000.

data.title: Bitcoin Market Awaits Potential Volatility Amid Global Economic Events
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the market is closely monitoring global macroeconomic events this week, including the European Central Bank's annual policy forum and U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's appearance on Tuesday. While Bitcoin's price remains relatively stable, signals from derivatives and on-chain markets suggest that volatility may soon return. Traders are paying attention to changes in major central bank policy statements, which could affect risk preferences in both crypto and traditional markets.

Analyst Axel Adler Jr. notes that whales are transferring significant funds to centralized trading platforms. This pattern, combined with declining platform reserves and reduced stablecoin inflows, typically indicates impending market volatility. As long as Bitcoin maintains its position above $108,000, the basic scenario is continued upward movement, with a target of $112,000.

data.title: Whale deposits another $16.51 million into HyperLiquid, using 5x leverage to go long on HYPE
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, two addresses suspected to belong to the same entity once again deposited a total of US$16.51 million to HyperLiquid and opened a HYPE long position with 5x leverage. Previously, the whale had accumulated a loss of US$5.6 million.

data.title: South Korea Halts CBDC Project Amid Rising Bank Concerns
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the Central Bank of South Korea has paused its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) project ahead of the second round of testing. This decision comes in response to increasing complaints from banks involved in the pilot project and growing discussions about introducing a Korean won stablecoin.

Currently, several banks, including KB Kookmin, Shinhan, Hana, Woori, NH Nonghyup, IBK Company, iM Bank, Suhyup, and K-Bank, are participating in projects focused on stablecoin issuance within the banking sector. These projects utilize open blockchain and DIDIA technology. Additionally, Busan Bank, Gyeongnam Bank, and Toss Bank are considering joining these initiatives.

The suspension reflects the need to address the concerns raised by the participating banks and to further evaluate the potential integration of a stablecoin pegged to the Korean won. The outcome of these discussions and evaluations will likely influence the future direction of South Korea's digital currency initiatives.

data.title: Significant Cryptocurrency Losses Reported Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
According to PANews, recent monitoring by @ai_9684xtpa has revealed substantial losses incurred by a trader known as 'Insider Brother.' Approximately one hour ago, the trader sold off 8,000 ETH and 280 BTC, amounting to a total position of $50.55 million, resulting in a loss of $1.011 million.

The current holdings of the trader include a 40x short position in BTC, with 1,120 BTC valued at $121 million, opened at a price of $106,808. Additionally, the trader holds a 25x short position in ETH, with 32,000 ETH valued at $80.34 million, opened at a price of $2,454.91.

These transactions highlight the ongoing volatility in the cryptocurrency market, as traders navigate fluctuating prices and market dynamics.

data.title: Planet Morning News
SEC Nears Decision on Grayscale Digital Large Cap ETF Approval
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Nate Geraci, President of The ETF Store, has indicated via social media that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is approaching the final approval deadline for the Grayscale Digital Large Cap ETF (GDLC) this week. The fund includes holdings in Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), XRP, Solana (SOL), and Cardano (ADA), with a high likelihood of approval.

Geraci suggests that if the GDLC is approved, it could pave the way for subsequent approvals of spot ETFs focused on individual assets such as XRP, SOL, and ADA. Approval of the GDLC would also serve as a beneficial trial for incorporating more cryptocurrency assets into the ETF framework.

Currently, XRP, SOL, and ADA collectively represent less than 10% of GDLC's holdings, offering regulators a gradual approach to engaging with other digital assets. This development could mark a significant step in the SEC's strategy to expand its oversight and integration of cryptocurrencies into traditional financial products.

data.title: NexVault will attend Japan IVS 2025 and release a new generation of Web3 financial operating system
Texas Recognizes Gold and Silver as Legal Tender for Daily Transactions
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Texas Governor Greg Abbott has signed legislation that designates gold and silver as legal tender for everyday transactions. This move allows residents to use these precious metals in daily commerce, aligning with efforts to diversify currency options within the state.

data.title: Bitcoin Magazine CEO: Plans to raise over $100 million to establish a Fairshake PAC-style organization to focus on promoting Bitcoin-related policies
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitcoin Magazine CEO David Bailey posted on the X platform that he is considering raising more than $100 million to establish a "Fairshake PAC"-style organization to focus on promoting Bitcoin-related policies. He warned that any lawmaker who votes against the Bitcoin tax amendment will face "retaliation" in the upcoming midterm elections.

data.title: HKEX to implement new stock settlement fee structure starting today
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Hong Kong Stock Exchange has implemented a new stock settlement fee structure since today (30th), completely breaking the original minimum limit of HK$2 and maximum limit of HK$100. "This adjustment is related to the policy of lowering the minimum price (the minimum price change unit for buying and selling stocks) in the Hong Kong market. Under the new standard, the cost of small transactions by investors will be greatly reduced." Industry insiders said. Specifically, when conducting small transactions in the past, the transaction cost was relatively high due to the existence of the minimum fee limit. For example, for a transaction of HK$1,000, the minimum fee is HK$2, and the fee ratio is as high as 0.2%. After the adjustment, the fee ratio is 0.0042%, or HK$0.042, and the cost is greatly reduced. At the same time, the cancellation of the minimum and maximum fee limits will enable institutional investors to calculate transaction costs more accurately and optimize trading strategies, which will help to better control costs when conducting large-scale transactions. In addition, after the new regulations, it is still charged on both sides of the transaction. (China Economic Net) (Jinshi)

data.title: Coinbase rose 43% in June, leading the S&P 500, and the stablecoin narrative boosted valuations
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to CNBC, Coinbase (COIN) rose 43% in June, becoming the best performing company in the S&P 500 index, and hit a high of $382 this week, setting a new high since its listing.
Analysis pointed out that this round of gains benefited from the advancement of the GENIUS Act (stablecoin regulatory legislation), which is expected to enhance Coinbase's stablecoin-related revenue capabilities. In particular, Coinbase's close relationship with USDC issuer Circle allows it to directly obtain platform USDC deposit income, as well as nearly half of other USDC income, which is equivalent to 99% of Circle's income. This means that shareholders can indirectly enjoy the revenue growth brought by Circle at almost no cost.
Although overall trading volume has continued to decline since April, the market's focus has shifted from traditional trading fees to stablecoin income and institutional narratives. As Washington's policies stabilize and Circle's stock price hits a new high, Coinbase's valuation logic is gradually moving away from the "exchange" attribute and moving closer to "compliant stablecoin financial infrastructure."
However, institutions remain vigilant about the rationality of its valuation, believing that there may be speculative factors in the current rise, and suggest paying attention to the risk of divergence between COIN and BTC. (CoinDesk)
